sources.notes: For each tree-path query, count colors that appear in every node's color set on the path.
Official editorial converts path intersections to intersections of color-component ID sets at the two endpoints, then balances small-set intersection with heavy-node precomputation.
Additional discussion of the sqrt bound for iterating over the smaller endpoint set across deduplicated queries.

annotation.summary: Answer tree path queries asking how many colors are present in every node on the path.
annotation.constraints: n,q,s are up to 3e5, so explicitly intersecting all color sets along a path is impossible.
annotation.core_idea: For each color, its vertices induce several connected components. Assign an ID to every maximal color component, and let D_u be the set of component IDs containing node u. A color appears on every node of path u-v iff u and v belong to the same color component, so the query answer is |D_u intersection D_v|. For small endpoint sets, intersect sorted component-ID lists with two pointers; for heavy endpoints, precompute all answers from that endpoint. Choosing threshold around sqrt(s) balances total work.
annotation.complexity: O((n+q+s) sqrt(s)) with O(s+n sqrt(s)) memory.
tricks: color components convert path intersection to endpoint intersection

solution_variants.name: component-set-sqrt-decomposition
solution_variants.summary: Build color-component ID sets for each node, precompute heavy endpoint intersections, and use two pointers for light endpoints.

solution_variants.name: small-set-query-bound
solution_variants.summary: Alternative implementation iterates the smaller endpoint set for deduplicated queries using the same min-size sum bound.

tags.evidence: The official solution chooses a threshold B and treats large endpoint sets with precomputation and small ones with direct intersection.
A DFS with per-color stacks assigns maximal connected color-component IDs on the tree.
Light queries intersect two sorted component-ID lists with two pointers.
The key reduction replaces each color's connected regions by component IDs, turning a path-wide intersection into an endpoint set intersection.
